agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics architecture using a region-primary ownership model with strict data residency enforcement. Each region (EU, US, APAC) runs an autonomous stack owning its customer and courier data, with a lightweight global coordination layer for cross-region metrics aggregation. Data residency is enforced at the routing layer (not just policy), region-local couriers are bound to region-local deliveries via aggregate invariants, and the global dashboard consumes pre-aggregated event streams rather than querying regional databases directly. Resilience is achieved through regional autonomy—each region can operate independently in degraded mode if global coordination fails.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Regional Gateway","responsibility":"Routes requests to the correct regional stack based on customer/courier region binding. Enforces data residency at the edge—requests for EU customers are ONLY routed to the EU stack. Provides <100ms latency by terminating TLS at regional PoPs.","boundaries":"One per region. No cross-region request forwarding for customer data. The gateway is the hard enforcement boundary for data residency—it is not advisory."},{"name":"Regional Application Stack","responsibility":"Fully autonomous service cluster handling orders, deliveries, courier assignment, and customer management for a single region. Contains all business logic. Can operate independently if global layer is unavailable.","boundaries":"Owns all customer PII, courier data, and delivery state for its region. No direct database access from other regions. Exposes only pre-aggregated metrics and anonymized operational events externally."},{"name":"Regional Database Cluster","responsibility":"Primary data store for the region. Contains customer PII, courier profiles, delivery records, and order history. Replicated within the region for HA (multi-AZ), never replicated cross-region.","boundaries":"Strict regional boundary. No cross-region replication of PII. Schema includes a region_id column on every table as a defense-in-depth check."},{"name":"Global Coordination Layer","responsibility":"Lightweight service that aggregates operational metrics from all regions, manages global configuration (pricing tiers, feature flags), and handles the rare cross-region workflows (e.g., a customer relocating between regions). Does NOT store or transit customer PII.","boundaries":"Runs in a designated primary region with failover. Only receives anonymized/aggregated data. Has no direct database connections to regional stores."},{"name":"Global Metrics Aggregator","responsibility":"Consumes pre-aggregated metric events from each region's event stream. Builds the unified global operations dashboard. Stores only operational metrics (delivery counts, SLA percentages, courier utilization)—never PII.","boundaries":"Read-only consumer of regional event streams. Data is aggregated and anonymized before leaving the region."},{"name":"Regional Event Bus","responsibility":"Intra-region async messaging for domain events (order placed, delivery assigned, courier location updated). Also publishes sanitized metric events to the global metrics stream.","boundaries":"Regional scope. PII-bearing events stay on the regional bus. Only metric/operational events are forwarded to the global stream after sanitization."},{"name":"Global DNS + Traffic Manager","responsibility":"GeoDNS routing that directs users to their nearest regional gateway. Provides failover routing within a region (e.g., EU primary to EU standby AZ) but NEVER fails over EU traffic to US.","boundaries":"Routing decisions respect data residency. A down EU region fails over to an EU standby, not to US or APAC."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Global DNS","to":"Regional Gateway","contract":"GeoDNS resolution based on client IP geolocation. Returns regional gateway IP. Override via explicit region header for internal tooling.","coupling_type":"infrastructure"},{"from":"Regional Gateway","to":"Regional Application Stack","contract":"HTTPS REST/gRPC. Gateway attaches verified region_id claim to every request. Application stack rejects requests where region_id doesn't match its own region.","coupling_type":"runtime"},{"from":"Regional Application Stack","to":"Regional Database Cluster","contract":"Connection pool within region VPC. All queries include region_id predicate as defense-in-depth. No cross-region DB connections exist at the network level (VPC peering is not configured for DB subnets).","coupling_type":"runtime"},{"from":"Regional Application Stack","to":"Regional Event Bus","contract":"Publishes domain events (OrderPlaced, DeliveryAssigned, CourierLocationUpdated). Schema registry enforced. Events contain region_id.","coupling_type":"async"},{"from":"Regional Event Bus","to":"Global Metrics Aggregator","contract":"Sanitization filter strips PII fields before forwarding. Only metric-class events (DeliveryCompleted, SLABreached, CourierUtilizationSnapshot) cross the boundary. Schema: {region, metric_type, value, timestamp}.","coupling_type":"async"},{"from":"Global Metrics Aggregator","to":"Global Operations Dashboard","contract":"WebSocket push for real-time updates. REST for historical queries. All data is aggregated—no drill-down to individual customer records.","coupling_type":"runtime"},{"from":"Global Coordination Layer","to":"Regional Application Stack","contract":"Configuration sync via pull model. Each region polls global config every 30s. If global layer is unreachable, regions use last-known config. No PII flows through this channel.","coupling_type":"async"}],"data_flow":"Customer request → GeoDNS resolves to regional gateway → gateway verifies region binding and routes to regional app stack → app stack processes against regional DB → domain events published to regional event bus → sanitization filter strips PII → metric events forwarded to global aggregator → global dashboard updated in real-time. Cross-region PII transfer is architecturally impossible: no network path exists between regional DB subnets, and the event sanitization filter is a hard gate.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Region-Primary Ownership (Cell Architecture)","rationale":"Each region is a self-contained 'cell' that owns its data and can operate autonomously. This is the only pattern that provides both data residency guarantees AND regional resilience simultaneously. Cells share nothing at the data layer."},{"pattern":"Event-Carried State Transfer (Sanitized)","rationale":"Cross-region communication uses events, not API calls or shared databases. Events are sanitized before leaving a region. This decouples regions temporally and prevents accidental PII leakage through synchronous call chains."},{"pattern":"Aggregate-Enforced Region Binding","rationale":"The Delivery aggregate enforces that courier.region_id == delivery.region_id as an invariant. This makes cross-region courier assignment a domain-level impossibility, not just a policy check."},{"pattern":"Defense in Depth for Data Residency","rationale":"Data residency is enforced at four layers: (1) DNS routing, (2) gateway region verification, (3) application-level region_id checks, (4) network-level VPC isolation. Any single layer can fail and residency is still maintained."},{"pattern":"Strangler Fig for Migration","rationale":"Existing EU-only system continues operating while US and APAC stacks are stood up. Traffic is gradually shifted via DNS weights. Allows incremental rollout with rollback capability."},{"pattern":"CQRS for Global Dashboard","rationale":"The global dashboard is a read-optimized projection built from event streams. It never queries regional databases directly. This separates the operational read path from the transactional write path and respects data boundaries."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Customer Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Customer (entity: identity, address, preferences; region_id is immutable after creation; value objects: ContactInfo, Address, DataConsentRecord)","CustomerRegionTransfer (saga: handles the rare case of a customer relocating; creates new customer in target region, archives in source region, never copies raw PII cross-region simultaneously)"]},{"name":"Courier Operations","classification":"core","aggregates":["Courier (entity: identity, certifications, vehicle; region_id is immutable; value objects: CourierProfile, OperatingZone, ShiftSchedule)","CourierAvailability (entity: real-time availability state; regional scope only)"]},{"name":"Delivery Fulfillment","classification":"core","aggregates":["Delivery (entity: tracks pickup→transit→delivered lifecycle; invariant: courier.region_id MUST equal delivery.region_id; value objects: DeliveryWindow, Route, ProofOfDelivery)","Order (entity: customer order; region_id inherited from customer; contains pickup/dropoff addresses that MUST be within region)"]},{"name":"Operational Metrics","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["RegionalMetricSnapshot (value object: pre-aggregated metrics per region per time window)","GlobalDashboardProjection (read model: unified view across regions, no PII)"]},{"name":"Identity & Access","classification":"generic","aggregates":["User (entity: authentication identity; region_id determines which regional stack they can access)","Role (value object: permissions scoped to region or global)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Customer Management","downstream":"Delivery Fulfillment","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Customer context publishes CustomerCreated events. Delivery context consumes to know valid customers and their region binding."},{"upstream":"Courier Operations","downstream":"Delivery Fulfillment","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Courier context owns courier availability. Delivery context queries available couriers within its region only."},{"upstream":"Delivery Fulfillment","downstream":"Operational Metrics","relationship":"Published Language. Delivery context publishes standardized metric events (DeliveryCompleted, SLABreached). Metrics context consumes without coupling to delivery internals."},{"upstream":"Identity & Access","downstream":"All Regional Contexts","relationship":"Conformist. Regional contexts conform to the global IAM model for authentication. Region-scoping of authorization is enforced locally."},{"upstream":"Regional Contexts","downstream":"Global Coordination Layer","relationship":"Open Host Service. Each region exposes a standardized metrics/config API. Global layer consumes without dictating regional internals."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Fully autonomous regional stacks (no shared database)","gains":"Bulletproof data residency compliance. Regional fault isolation. Independent deployability per region. Sub-100ms latency guaranteed.","sacrifices":"Higher infrastructure cost (3x the compute/DB). More complex deployment pipeline. Customer region transfer is a multi-step saga instead of a simple update.","alternatives_considered":["Single global database with row-level security — rejected: cannot guarantee data residency at the infrastructure level; a misconfigured query exposes cross-region PII","Active-active multi-region with CRDTs — rejected: unnecessary complexity; data doesn't need to be eventually consistent across regions because it's region-bound by design"]},{"decision":"Pre-aggregated metrics instead of direct cross-region queries","gains":"Zero risk of PII leakage through the dashboard. Dashboard latency is independent of regional DB performance. Regions can be down without breaking the dashboard (stale data shown).","sacrifices":"Dashboard data has 5-15 second latency vs real-time. Cannot drill down to individual deliveries from the global view (must switch to regional view).","alternatives_considered":["Federated query engine across regions — rejected: creates a network path to regional DBs that could leak PII; adds latency; violates cell isolation"]},{"decision":"Immutable region_id on Customer and Courier aggregates","gains":"Prevents accidental cross-region data movement. Simplifies every query (always scoped to region). Makes compliance auditing trivial.","sacrifices":"Customer relocation requires a saga (create new + archive old) instead of an update. Cannot support couriers who operate across regions.","alternatives_considered":["Mutable region_id with audit log — rejected: too easy to accidentally move data cross-region; audit log is a detective control, we need a preventive one"]},{"decision":"Defense-in-depth data residency (4 layers)","gains":"Any single layer can fail or be misconfigured without a compliance breach. Provides auditable evidence of residency controls for regulators.","sacrifices":"More operational complexity. Slightly higher latency from additional checks (negligible—adds <2ms). Risk of false positives blocking legitimate requests if layers disagree.","alternatives_considered":["Single enforcement point at the gateway — rejected: single point of failure for compliance; one bug = regulatory breach"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions","evolution_path":"The cell architecture makes adding a new region (e.g., LATAM, Middle East) a deployment exercise, not an architectural change. Stand up a new cell, configure DNS, done. The global coordination layer discovers new regions via service registry."},{"area":"Per-region regulatory customization","evolution_path":"Each regional stack can diverge in business logic (e.g., APAC may need different delivery window rules). Since stacks are autonomous, regional customization doesn't affect other regions."},{"area":"Global dashboard evolution","evolution_path":"Because the dashboard consumes events, new metrics can be added by publishing new event types. No changes to regional databases or APIs needed."},{"area":"Technology heterogeneity","evolution_path":"Regional stacks could run different database engines or service implementations if needed (e.g., APAC on a different cloud provider for data sovereignty). The only contract is the event schema."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Region binding on aggregates","reason":"Intentionally rigid. Customer.region_id and Courier.region_id are immutable by design. Changing this would undermine every data residency guarantee. This rigidity IS the compliance architecture."},{"area":"Event sanitization boundary","reason":"The PII stripping filter between regional and global event streams must be rigid. Adding a new field to cross-region events requires explicit review to ensure no PII leakage. This friction is a feature."},{"area":"No cross-region database connectivity","reason":"VPC network configuration intentionally blocks cross-region DB access. This is a physical network constraint, not just a policy. Changing it requires infrastructure-level changes with compliance sign-off."},{"area":"GeoDNS region assignment","reason":"Customer-to-region mapping is determined at account creation and encoded in DNS routing rules. Changing a customer's region is a deliberate, multi-step process. This prevents accidental cross-region serving."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Global coordination layer becomes a single point of failure for config distribution","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Regions cache last-known config and operate independently for up to 24 hours without global coordination. Config changes during an outage are queued and applied on reconnection."},{"risk":"Event sanitization filter has a bug that leaks PII to the global metrics stream","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Defense in depth: (1) Schema registry rejects events with PII-typed fields on the global stream, (2) automated PII scanning on the global metrics store runs hourly, (3) sanitization filter has 100% unit test coverage with PII detection assertions."},{"risk":"Customer region transfer saga fails midway, leaving customer data in two regions temporarily","severity":"high","mitigation":"Saga uses reservation pattern: new region record is created in 'pending' state, old region record is marked 'transferring'. Only after new region confirms readiness is old record archived. Timeout after 1 hour triggers automatic rollback. During transfer, customer sees read-only mode."},{"risk":"Clock skew between regions causes metric aggregation anomalies on global dashboard","severity":"low","mitigation":"All events carry server-side UTC timestamps from NTP-synced sources. Aggregator uses event time, not processing time. Dashboard shows data staleness indicator per region."},{"risk":"EU degraded mode provides inconsistent reads during regional failover","severity":"high","mitigation":"EU runs across 3 AZs. Read replicas in each AZ. If primary AZ fails, a read replica is promoted within the same region. Customers may see stale data for 15-30 seconds during promotion. Write operations return 503 with retry-after header during failover window."},{"risk":"Latency budget exceeded for APAC customers due to limited infrastructure in some sub-regions","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Deploy APAC PoPs in key locations (Tokyo, Singapore, Sydney). Use CDN for static assets. Keep <100ms target for API calls by ensuring database read replicas are in each major sub-region."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase the rollout using Strangler Fig. Phase 1: Extract the existing EU monolith into the cell architecture pattern (EU cell). Phase 2: Stand up US cell as a fresh deployment, migrate US-based customers. Phase 3: Stand up APAC cell. Phase 4: Deploy global coordination layer and dashboard. Each phase is independently valuable and de-risks the next. Start by defining the event schema and sanitization contract—these are the hardest to change later.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/regions/{eu,us,apac}/terraform/ — per-region infrastructure-as-code","services/gateway/region-router.ts — request routing with region verification","services/delivery/domain/delivery.aggregate.ts — Delivery aggregate with courier-region invariant enforcement","services/customer/domain/customer.aggregate.ts — Customer aggregate with immutable region_id","services/events/sanitization-filter.ts — PII stripping for cross-region event forwarding","services/events/schemas/ — event schema definitions with PII field annotations","services/metrics-aggregator/ — global metrics consumer and dashboard projection","services/global-coordination/ — config distribution and region registry","infra/vpc/ — per-region VPC configuration with explicit cross-region isolation","docs/adr/001-multi-region-cell-architecture.md","docs/adr/002-data-residency-defense-in-depth.md","docs/adr/003-event-sanitization-contract.md","docs/runbook/customer-region-transfer.md","docs/runbook/regional-failover.md"],"patterns_to_follow":"Cell Architecture for regional isolation. Aggregate invariants for business rule enforcement. Event-Carried State Transfer for cross-region communication. CQRS for the global dashboard. Defense in Depth for compliance. Saga pattern for cross-region workflows. All PII fields annotated in schema with @pii decorator. Every database query must include region_id in WHERE clause (enforced by ORM middleware). Integration tests must verify that no PII appears in global event stream.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT create cross-region database replication for customer/courier data—even read replicas. This violates data residency.","Do NOT use a global API gateway that inspects request bodies—the gateway should route based on headers/tokens only, never parse PII.","Do NOT allow the global dashboard to drill down to individual records—this creates a temptation to pipe PII to the global layer.","Do NOT implement region transfer as a simple database record move—use the saga pattern to ensure atomicity and rollback capability.","Do NOT hardcode region lists—use a service registry so new regions can be added without code changes.","Do NOT skip the sanitization filter for 'internal' events—all events crossing a regional boundary must be sanitized, no exceptions.","Do NOT use eventually-consistent cross-region replication and claim it satisfies data residency because 'data is mostly in the right region'—residency must be absolute."]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Cell architecture is the correct pattern for data residency + regional autonomy","Defense-in-depth for compliance enforcement","Aggregate-level invariants for courier-region binding","Event sanitization as the cross-region communication boundary","CQRS for global dashboard"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact latency characteristics for APAC sub-regions without knowing the cloud provider and PoP locations","Customer region transfer saga—exact timeout and rollback semantics need domain expert input","Whether 5-15 second dashboard latency meets the 'real-time' requirement—may need clarification from stakeholders"],"assumptions":["Cloud provider supports multi-AZ deployments in all three target regions","Couriers are strictly regional and never operate across region boundaries (no edge case for border regions)","Customer region is determined at account creation time and rarely changes","The 'real-time' requirement for the global dashboard tolerates seconds of latency, not sub-second","Existing EU system can be refactored into the cell pattern without a full rewrite"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics architecture using a region-primary ownership model with strict data residency enforcement. Each region (EU, US, APAC) runs an autonomous stack owning its customer and courier data, with a lightweight global coordination layer for cross-region metrics aggregation. Data residency is enforced at the routing layer (not just policy), region-local couriers are bound to region-local deliveries via aggregate invariants, and the global dashboard consumes pre-
